name: Clifford Brown
abstract: Clifford Benjamin Brown (October 30, 1930 – June 26, 1956) was an American jazz trumpeter, pianist, and composer. He died at the age of 25 in a car crash, leaving behind four years' worth of recordings.

concerts.label: Jazz and Variety Concert to Benefit The Lighthouse

works.t: Anything Goes: I Get a Kick Out of You

about_quotes.speaker: Branford Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: And you listen to him on Unity and Woody was figuring it out. Then you put the records on and the solos, you know, kind of like the way Clifford Brown used to play, not as fluid in that direction.
about_quotes.doc_title: Branford Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Brunious
about_quotes.evidence: I always heard traditional music... I mean I always heard... Clifford Brown quite naturally... Clifford Brown used to wear that thing out... Clifford Brown displayed... how to play melody.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Brunious interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Sheryl Bailey
about_quotes.evidence: I had forgotten that I’d done all that work... there was a ton of Clifford Brown... Clifford Brown to me I think is the most impeccable, perfect soloist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sheryl Bailey interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 24, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Davis
about_quotes.evidence: I listened to a lot of Blue Note records... Clifford Brown, Max Roach... I wasn’t even really thinking about the trombone so much, just the language.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, December 15, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Sheldon
about_quotes.evidence: If you had to list a few of the trumpet players that influenced you pretty strongly in your formative years, who would they be? ... Clifford Brown.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Sheldon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Hey
about_quotes.evidence: he heard Clifford Brown live in 1954 I believe, in Chicago... So I had that, and then as Clifford Brown records came out my dad would buy those
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Hey interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 2, 2023
